claim: EVA vowel patterns are non-randomly distributed across manuscript sections at the aggregate level (chi-square significant at p<0.01 in 55/70 testable skeleton groups) but this distributional coupling does NOT translate to per-token section prediction on held-out folios. The narrower surviving result: sparse high-precision vowel-pattern rules exist (e.g. '_.eo' is pharma-modal with rule-precision 0.827 on held-out data) but fire on only ~40% of tokens, so F1 is below always-predict-majority baselines.
evidence_for: 55/70 skeleton groups section-coupled at p<0.01 (aggregate chi-square)
Held-out rule table precision 0.827 for pharmaceutical when the rule fires
Held-out NB precision 0.655 for biological vs 0.557 prevalence (+10pp)
kdy case holds: e-vowel variants all biological, o-vowel variants pharma/herbal
evidence_against: Pharma F1 0.341 (best) vs blind 0.614 on held-out data
Bio F1 0.658 (best) vs blind 0.716 on held-out data
Multi-class NB accuracy 43.6% vs majority baseline 55.7%
Per-folio: held-out pharma folios mis-classified as biological at 75%+ rate
Rule coverage only 40% of held-out tokens - most tokens have no applicable rule
test: Held-out validation with 4 unseen folios (f101r, f89r2, f78r, f82r) was the pre-registered falsifier for H-BV-VOWEL-CODE-01. That failed. This narrower hypothesis passes aggregate chi-square but fails per-token F1.
test_threshold: Chi-square signif. AND heldout precision > prevalence + 10pp on at least one target section

tests_run.details: Aggregate chi-square unchanged (55/70 at p<0.01). Heldout precision 0.827 (pharma, rule fires) and 0.655 (bio, NB) both exceed prevalence baseline. F1 fails, precision survives.

tests_remaining: Build sparse high-precision dictionary of rule-fires-only patterns
Test whether image-labeled content (vs section metadata) is the right target
reasoning: Honest successor to H-BV-VOWEL-CODE-01 after that hypothesis failed its pre-registered held-out test. The aggregate chi-square signal is real. The per-token prediction is not. The right future path is sparse high-precision mappings, not broad classifiers.
